prompt: Use domain-knowledge-distiller to absorb this article into work_contexts.
expected_evidence: Loads the skill, identifies domain, writes or proposes a domain mini wiki update, includes provenance and retrieval keys.

prompt: This failure case is valuable. Turn it into principles I can use later when building products.
expected_evidence: Selects durable distillation behavior, extracts mechanism and boundaries, creates future-use knowledge cards.

prompt: Here are a few quotes, a rough architecture, and a failed launch story. Help me internalize the reusable philosophy and make future agents able to call it efficiently.
expected_evidence: Handles multiple source types, routes into principles, architecture, micro tactics, casebook, and retrieval index.

prompt: I already have a work_context for agent workflows. Add this source so future Codex can find it when I ask about routing failures.
expected_evidence: Updates existing context, patches index/retrieval, and performs a retrieval pressure check.
Does not write durable work_context knowledge or require knowledge cards.

prompt: Translate this paragraph into Chinese and keep the tone elegant.
Absorb this but I do not know which domain it belongs to.
expected_evidence: Asks one concise domain question or makes a clearly stated low-risk default if inferable.

prompt: This viral thread feels insightful. Decide whether it deserves durable memory and extract only what is actually reusable.
expected_evidence: Uses source quality rubric, rejects or downgrades weak claims, stores uncertainties in open_questions.

prompt: Absorb these sources into /tmp/example-memory/work_contexts/sales_training instead of the default memory folder.
expected_evidence: Uses the user-provided target path instead of a hardcoded personal path.

prompt: These two high-quality sources disagree. Preserve both mechanisms and make the future agent aware of the unresolved tradeoff.
expected_evidence: Routes source tension to open_questions or a conflict-aware pattern instead of forcing false consensus.

prompt: Before you say the domain context is complete, validate source refs, derived pages, frontmatter, and manifest shape.
expected_evidence: Runs lint_domain_context.py and reports any schema or cross-reference failures.

prompt: Add this as a new work_context and make sure future agents can route to it without relying on my local core/router files.
expected_evidence: Updates contexts.registry.json, root README, the context README, router evals, open risks, and runs validate_work_contexts.py.

prompt: This generated index has /Users/me/... paths and many duplicate pattern names from different repos. Make the knowledge base portable and less noisy.
expected_evidence: Converts retrieval metadata to repo-relative paths, adds or updates a canonical grouping index, and validates the work_contexts root.
